sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking (48, 39) from (6, 17) didn't do anything. The white diamond stayed at (6, 17). So (48, 39) is not a valid destination from the starting position.
Let me think about what's different between the first run and this run. In the first run:
- The white diamond started at (6, 17)
- I clicked (48, 54), (35, 45), (21, 8), (9, 49) - white diamond moved to each
- Then I clicked (48, 39) and the white diamond moved there AND other things moved
But in this run, after clicking the same sequence, clicking (48, 39) didn't work.
Wait, maybe the issue is that in the first run, I hadn't clicked (6, 17) yet. In this run, I clicked (6, 17) after visiting all other destinations. Maybe that "reset" something.
Let me try resetting the level and trying the exact same sequence as the first run without visiting (6, 17).
Actually, let me try clicking (32, 32) to reset, then try the sequence (48, 54), (35, 45), (21, 8), (9, 49), (48, 39) again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Reset and try the original sequence again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 32}])

# Check state after reset
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After reset:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] < 30 and n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'p', 'O', 'M']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
